quotes.verbatim_quote: One of the many uses of triads is in harmonizing simple tunes like Christmas carols.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Frames a major use-case for the triad chapter.

quotes.topic: 7th chord as triad: omit root or 5th
quotes.verbatim_quote: When a 7th chord triad is desired, it is usually best to leave out the root or Sth.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Voicing rule for reducing 7th chord to triad form.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The technique of substituting the relative minor is often used on
the IV chord of a key
quotes.why_load_bearing: Specific application of relative-substitution at IV.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
the V7 (or sometimes V) chord almost always
is the chord that “sets up” the new key; without some form of V7 or V chord, the transition to a new
key sounds awkward.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Modulation principle: V7 is required setup chord.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Open voicing refers to any arrangement of tones other than closed voicing.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines open voicing as complement to closed.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
WHEN
USING OPEN TRIADS, THE ROOT IS USALLY PUT IN THE BASS FOR THE LAST CHORD IN
A PHRASE
quotes.why_load_bearing: Voicing rule for phrase endings using open triads.

quotes.verbatim_quote: To make iiads into 4 note chords, you may double a root, 3rd, or 5th.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Rule for promoting triad to 4-note voicing via doubling.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Every scale has fundamental harmonies or chords that can be built from it.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Frames the harmonized-scale concept central to Section 16.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
THE NEXT CHORD
IS DERIVED BY MOVING EACH TONE IN THE IST CHORD UP TO THE NEXT TONE IN THE
SCALE.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Mechanical rule for constructing diatonic chord series.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The names of the chords are derived BY THINKING OF THE BASS NOTE OF EACH CHORD AS
THE ROOT
quotes.why_load_bearing: Naming rule for derived diatonic chords.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
important point is that a VII diminished triad sounds like a dominant 7th chord whose root is 2
whole steps lower.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Identifies vii° as rootless V7 — a key substitution insight.

quotes.verbatim_quote: You would then have the following chords: I+ m7 Im? IV# V7 VIm7 V. Im7>5 VIIIF.
